title: cassipourea elliptica
intro: Cassipourea elliptica, also known as the Cassipourea tree, is a medicinal plant species native to tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Cassipourea elliptica include the treatment of fever, skin infections, and wounds, as well as its use as an anti-diarrheal and anti-spasmodic agent. The plant is typically prepared as a decoction, infusion, or ointment, with the leaves and bark being used in various herbal preparations.
